patch.value: Stale in-progress detection: validate/health flag in_progress items with no active claim or recent activity
Abandoned-work hygiene for multi-agent workspaces: surface in_progress items that no agent is actually working on, so pm next/context and human dashboards reflect true WIP.

pm validate (and/or pm health) gains a warn-only check flagging in_progress items that have no active claim AND no history activity within a configurable staleness window (default ~72h); output lists item IDs with last-activity timestamps and a remediation hint (claim, release back to open, or justify); pm update --status in_progress without an existing claim emits a one-line advisory suggesting pm claim; window configurable via settings; docs + tests; zero behavior change for existing green workflows (warn-only)

## Evidence (audit #14, 2026-07-06)
The docstring initiative (epic pm-4ak1 + 8 child tasks) was bulk-set to in_progress on 2026-07-04/05 and then abandoned: no claim on any item, no branch, no PR, no commit touches docstrings (verified via git branch -a / gh pr list / git log --grep docstring). For 2 days pm next, pm context, and stats reported 9 phantom active work items.

## Why this matters at scale
The scale story (pm-9rxu, pm-g2zz) assumes thousands of concurrent agents; with advisory-only claims and no staleness detection, every crashed or wandering agent permanently poisons the WIP signal. pm claim today has no TTL/expiry and validate has no WIP-accuracy check — status honesty is entirely manual.

## Sketch
- validate check id: validate_claims_stale_in_progress (warn): in_progress AND no active claim AND last history entry older than window (settings key, default 72h).
- Advisory on 'pm update --status in_progress' when the item is unclaimed: 'hint: pm claim <id> --author <you> to signal ownership'.
- Related-not-duplicate: pm-zwib (stale LOCK takeover race — file locks, not work claims), pm-8t5x closed (claim atomicity), pm-114v (claim --next distribution).

Duplicate check: searched stale/abandoned/in_progress/claim/lease/heartbeat — no existing item covers WIP-staleness detection.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Live evidence 2026-07-10 (first pass with PR#502 caller-aware pm next in place): 9 open items claimed 2026-07-04 by maintainer-agent (docstring umbrella pm-4ak1, gate pm-5566, + 7 module slices pm-m0uc/mswi/uxmf/uwu0/768v/2vb2/qely) sat dormant ~6 days with zero work. Effect measured: anonymous pm next callers saw 8 of them under held_by_others as apparent live lanes and they were excluded from the shared ready pool; with PM_AUTHOR=maintainer-agent the recommender steered the owner to resume pm-5566 instead of frontier work (pm-3mna). A claim-TTL/staleness signal would have surfaced these for release days earlier. Hygiene applied this pass: the 9 claims released (items stay open, statuses untouched). Foreign lanes pm-tnud (codex-agent, 07-07) and pm-92if (claude-code-agent) left alone; pm-92if release-then-reclaim sequence verified consistent, no state defect.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Stale-state reclamation pair: claim TTL expiry and stale-lock recovery share detection semantics

author: claude-code-agent
message: Start broad SDK/CLI mutation-integrity implementation: provenance enforcement, history completeness, secret guard, and stale-WIP governance share one acceptance surface.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Link stale-work health to shared mutation policy primitives

value.note: installed CLI advisory and JSON contract proof
installed CLI advisory and JSON contract proof

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-07-24: shipped public pure/filesystem stale-work SDK scans plus advisory health integration using item and latest valid history activity, configurable default 72h. Direct human pm update <id> --status in_progress now warns when the resulting item is unassigned; --json suppresses presentation advice to preserve parseable machine stderr. Fresh packed-install acceptance observed the warning and passed health/validate. Linked 35-test item gate and full 6208-test exact 100/100/100/100 coverage passed.

patch.value: Configurable SDK stale-WIP detection and non-disruptive CLI/health advisories implemented for multi-agent ownership honesty.
Old unclaimed active items are listed with timestamps/remediation and direct unclaimed in-progress transitions advise agents to claim work without changing green workflow semantics.
Health emits deterministic advisory details, human updates advise pm claim, JSON remains parseable, and installed-package acceptance passes.
Implemented and verified configurable stale unclaimed-work scans plus human update ownership advice and JSON compatibility.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: CodeRabbit follow-up: stale-work scanning now degrades a single corrupt or unreadable item history stream to metadata-only activity instead of failing the whole Promise.all health scan. Added corrupt-JSON regression coverage; focused SDK governance tests pass.
